source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
At Almetra, we’re on a mission to reinvent how factories work.

We help manufacturers - one of the most essential and overlooked sectors of our economy - run better. Every car, wind turbine, medical device, and washing machine comes from a factory. But factories today are running out of people. They struggle with efficiency, safety, and quality - and they desperately need better tools to understand what’s happening on the shop floor.

That’s where we come in. Almetra builds AI-powered computer vision systems that give manufacturers deep visibility into how their production lines actually run - and the insights to improve them. We don’t sell ads or marketing software. We help real factories solve real problems.

You’ll be joining at a pivotal moment as we scale from an early-stage startup into a category-defining company.

Why join us

 - Mission with real impact. You’ll help the world’s most essential companies stay competitive, sustainable, and safe in a world of labor shortages and rising costs.

 - Category-defining moment. With global manufacturers already deploying Almetra and fresh funding, we’re scaling fast across Europe and the US.

 - Big deals, big influence. You’ll lead 6-digit enterprise sales cycles, shape our go-to-market motion, and be a core driver of our revenue engine.

 - High bar, high trust. You’ll work directly with founders, product, and engineering to design solutions and expand strategic accounts.

What you’ll do

 - Run full-cycle 6-digit enterprise deals across Europe and North America.

 - Partner with SDRs, Growth, and Almetra Engineers to land and expand high-value accounts.

 - Build trust with leaders in operations, engineering, quality, and IT/OT.

 - Lead sharp discovery and clearly show how Almetra transforms factory performance.

 - Navigate complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise cycles (legal, security, procurement).

 - Drive expansions and help shape our repeatable GTM playbook.

 - Maintain tight pipeline hygiene, forecast accurately, and consistently hit your number.

What we’re looking for

 - 4+ years of full-cycle enterprise sales with a track record of beating quota.

 - Hungry, gritty, fast - you own deals and outcomes.

 - Proven ability to run complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise cycles.

 - Strong negotiator, confident with redlines, legal, and security reviews.

 - Comfortable visiting plants and learning how factories actually run.

 - Disciplined with CRM, forecasting, and deal management.

 - Team player across Growth, SDRs, Product, CS, and Engineering.

 - Familiar with GDPR and enterprise onboarding requirements.

Bonus points

 - Experience selling AI, automation, robotics, or advanced manufacturing solutions.

 - Background in operations, production, or industrial engineering (formally or through customer exposure).

 - Interest in building and operationalizing a repeatable enterprise GTM playbook.

 - German or English fluent speaker.

What we offer

Employee Share Options Program for all permanent employees*

An increasing benefits list: currently includes Urban Sports club and quarterly team retreats.

Be on the forefront in defining what artificial intelligence means in manufacturing

Gain hands-on experience in working in an AI-first software company

Supportive and inclusive culture that values diversity and promotes the advancement of underrepresented groups within the company

Collaborate with a diverse (currently more than 10 nationalities) and talented team, working on cutting-edge projects with real-world impact

Network with professionals and leaders in the field, opening doors to potential future career opportunities

We have a very flat hierarchy, open 360° feedback, and flexible working hours

Ethics⚖: We are committed to developing ethical AI software

Don't meet all the requirements?

Almetra is committed to creating a workplace that is diverse, fair, and inclusive. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds, even if they do not meet every qualificat

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff Robotics Engineer (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
Join us in reshaping manufacturing with advanced tech like computer vision and AI! 

As a Senior/Staff Robotics Engineer (m/f/d), you will be integral in driving our company's next phase of innovation - automating industrial processes to address the scarcity of skilled labor. We have already proven our ability to optimize processes and increase efficiency, and now we are determined to automate tasks within industrial environments. Your main focus will be on R&D and will involve building and showcasing a demonstrator that utilizes industrial robot arms to automate currently manual tasks. 

You have a strong background in state of the art in robotic manipulation, extensive experience in modeling, planning, and controlling robotic systems, as well as applying machine learning techniques to enhance robotic manipulation capabilities.



Your role

 - Lead R&D efforts to prototype a new robotic manipulator, conducting rigorous testing to validate design specifications and performance criteria.

 - Work with industry-standard hardware platforms (e.g., ROS-compatible robots, specific sensor suites) to develop and deploy robotic manipulators for real world testing. 

 - Develop and implement models for hybrid dynamical systems exhibiting both continuous and discrete dynamic behaviors, specifically in robotic manipulators.

 - Design advanced planning algorithms to optimize manipulation tasks such as pick-and-place operations.

 - Experience with supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning techniques to improve manipulation performance in robots.

 - Previously trained RL models in both simulated and real-world environments with continuous action and state spaces.

 - Design and develop middleware and software solutions tailored for robotic manipulation applications.

 - Optimize software architecture for efficient communication between sensors, processors, and actuators in robotic manipulators.

 - Develop perception algorithms for object pose estimation, matching, and instance segmentation to aid in manipulation tasks.

 - Utilize deep learning techniques for end-to-end and self-supervised grasping, enhancing the robot's autonomous manipulation capabilities.

 - Experience with integration between hardware components, including tactile sensors, force sensors, vision systems (e.g., RGB-D cameras), and depth sensors to optimize communication protocols and data flow for efficient robotic operations.



Your profile

 - Ph.D. in Robotics, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or related field, with a strong emphasis on robotics.

 - Minimum of 1-2 years of experience in robotics research or development.

 - Proven experience in modeling, planning, and controlling hybrid dynamical systems for robotic manipulation.

 - Hands-on experience with machine learning techniques applied to robotic manipulation tasks.

 - Experience with robotic perception technologies and deep learning frameworks as applied to manipulation.

 - Strong programming skills in C++, Python, and familiarity with ROS (Robot Operating System).

 - Experience with simulation tools such as Nvidia Isaac SIM, Gazebo, V-REP, CUDA for robotic manipulation.

 - Proficiency with machine learning libraries like TensorFlow or PyTorch.

 - Fluency in English is mandatory.

What we offer

Employee Share Options Program for all permanent employees*

An increasing benefits list: currently includes Urban Sports club and quarterly team retreats.

Be on the forefront in defining what artificial intelligence means in manufacturing

Gain hands-on experience in working in an AI-first software company

Supportive and inclusive culture that values diversity and promotes the advancement of underrepresented groups within the company

Collaborate with a diverse (currently more than 10 nationalities) and talented team, working on cutting-edge projects with real-world impact

Network with professionals and leaders in the field, opening doors to

source_jobs.title: Manufacturing Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a Manufacturing Data Analyst who combines solid analytical and coding skills with a strong understanding of industrial operations. You’ll work directly with customers and internal teams to translate manufacturing data into actionable insights — improving both customer outcomes and our AI analytics platform.

What you’ll do

 - Collaborate with manufacturing customers to analyse production data and uncover performance drivers

 - Work closely with AI and backend engineers to define, test and refine data pipelines, features and quality metrics

 - Build clear, reliable dashboards and reports that highlight process efficiency, bottlenecks and improvement areas

 - Identify anomalies or optimisation opportunities that feed back into our product’s analytics layer

 - Write clean, well-structured code for data processing and analysis — maintainable, reviewable, and production-aware

 - Act as the bridge between Customer Success, Product and Engineering — turning operational insights into measurable impact

You are

 - 3–5 years in data analytics or process optimisation, ideally in manufacturing, automotive, pharma or other high-volume industries

 - Strong in SQL and Python (or similar) — able to write clean, efficient, reusable code

 - Experienced working with production data, sensors, or process control systems

 - Analytical mindset with understanding of data modelling, feature extraction and algorithmic thinking

 - Comfortable communicating findings to both technical and operational stakeholders

 - Curious, structured and pragmatic — you solve real problems, not just analyse them

Nice to have

 - Background in process engineering, lean manufacturing, or industrial analytics

 - Familiarity with manufacturing software (MES, ERP, SCADA, etc.)

 - Experience integrating analytics into customer-facing workflows

What we offer

Employee Share Options Program for all permanent employees*

An increasing benefits list: currently includes Urban Sports club and quarterly team retreats.

Be on the forefront in defining what artificial intelligence means in manufacturing

Gain hands-on experience in working in an AI-first software company

Supportive and inclusive culture that values diversity and promotes the advancement of underrepresented groups within the company

Collaborate with a diverse (currently more than 10 nationalities) and talented team, working on cutting-edge projects with real-world impact

Network with professionals and leaders in the field, opening doors to potential future career opportunities

We have a very flat hierarchy, open 360° feedback, and flexible working hours

Ethics⚖: We are committed to developing ethical AI software

Don't meet all the requirements?

Almetra is committed to creating a workplace that is diverse, fair, and inclusive. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds, even if they do not meet every qualification, to submit their application. We firmly believe that having a team with diverse perspectives only strengthens our company and drives innovation. Our commitment also extends to providing an accessible environment for everyone, including those with disabilities. Please let us know if you require any accommodations during the application process or while working with us, and we will do our best to support you.


*Only full-time, permanent roles are eligible for stock options. Part-time roles, contract roles, work-student, internships and freelance roles are not eligible for stock options.

source_jobs.title: Senior Computer Vision Engineer (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a Computer Vision Engineer focused on bringing advanced vision models into production. You’ll own the full lifecycle of CV systems — from model integration and orchestration to deployment on cloud and edge environments.



YOUR ROLE

 - Develop and deploy computer vision models for real-world video and sensor applications

 - Build reliable training, evaluation and inference pipelines for large-scale data

 - Operate and optimise GPU clusters, Docker/Kubernetes environments and cloud workloads

 - Implement CI/CD, versioning and orchestration for ML pipelines

 - Collaborate with research engineers to transition prototypes into scalable, production-grade systems

 - Work with robotics and platform teams to ensure robust deployment at the edge


YOU ARE

 - 5–7 years experience in computer vision, ML engineering or production AI systems

 - Strong in Python, PyTorch and modern CV architectures (e.g. temporal transformers, detection, tracking)

 - Experienced with containerisation and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes, MLFlow, Airflow, etc.)

 - Familiar with distributed training, GPU management and inference optimisation

 - Solid understanding of cloud infrastructure (AWS or similar) and MLOps tooling

 - Pragmatic engineer — focused on reliability, reproducibility and maintainability


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience deploying CV models on edge devices or embedded systems

 - Familiarity with TensorRT, quantisation, and real-time inference optimisation

 - Background in video analytics, action recognition or multi-modal perception

 - Understanding of model orchestration frameworks and large data pipelines



WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS


You bridge research and production — turning cutting-edge computer vision models into robust, real-time systems that power intelligent industrial automation.

What we offer

Employee Share Options Program for all permanent employees*

An increasing benefits list: currently includes Urban Sports club and quarterly team retreats.

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative (AI Startup)
source_jobs.description:
At Almetra, we’re on a mission to reinvent how factories work.

We help manufacturers - one of the most essential and overlooked sectors of our economy - run better. Every car, wind turbine, medical device, and washing machine comes from a factory. But factories today are running out of people. They struggle with efficiency, safety, and quality - and they desperately need better tools to understand what’s happening on the shop floor.

That’s where we come in. Almetra builds AI-powered computer vision systems that give manufacturers deep visibility into how their production lines actually run - and the insights to improve them. We don’t sell ads or marketing software. We help real factories solve real problems.

You’ll be joining at a pivotal moment as we scale from an early-stage startup into a category-defining company.




WHY JOIN US

 - Mission with real impact. You’ll help the world’s most important companies - the ones that actually make things - stay competitive and sustainable in the face of labor shortages and rising costs.

 - Rocketship growth. With fresh Series A funding and global manufacturers as customers, we’re entering a massive growth phase.

 - Career acceleration. Clear path to Account Executive within 12-15 months. You’ll learn to sell deep-tech, AI-driven enterprise deals with six-digit ACVs.

 - Tight team, high standards. You’ll work directly with our founders and AEs, learning the craft of enterprise sales from the ground up.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Identify and reach out to potential manufacturing customers across Europe.

 - Start conversations with operations, production, and quality leaders - the people running factories day to day.

 - Qualify leads and book meetings for our Account Executives.

 - Partner closely with your AE to shape pipeline, messaging, and market feedback.

 - Help us scale our go-to-market playbook as we grow.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 1-3 years in B2B sales or sales development (software experience helps, but curiosity and drive matter more).

 - You move fast, take initiative, and have grit.

 - Strong communication skills - written, spoken, and over the phone.

 - Comfortable talking to engineers, production managers, and factory leaders.

 - Organized and disciplined - you keep clean notes and know your numbers.

 - Goal-oriented and motivated by learning and growth.




BONUS POINTS

 - Experience with tools like HubSpot, Sales Navigator, Lemlist, or Aircall.

 - Familiarity with automation tools (Clay, n8n, or similar).

 - Interest in AI, robotics, or manufacturing tech.



Let’s build the future of manufacturing together.

source_jobs.title: Field Installation Engineer (AI Startup)
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a hands-on engineer to own the deployment of our AI edge systems at customer sites. You’ll assemble, configure, and install hardware cabinets, networking equipment, and camera systems — ensuring everything runs smoothly in real industrial environments.

Your Role

 - Prepare and install full edge setups — cameras, compute nodes, and networking components

 - Configure devices, test connectivity, and verify data flow from edge to cloud

 - Develop and use automation scripts for faster installation, calibration, and verification

 - Troubleshoot and resolve on-site issues across hardware, network, and software layers

 - Coordinate component sourcing and logistics with the operations team

 - Ensure every installation meets reliability, safety, and performance standards

Your Profile

 - 3+ years in industrial hardware installation, systems integration, or field engineering

 - Strong practical experience with Linux, networking, and device configuration

 - Familiarity with edge computing, camera systems, or IoT deployments

 - Proficiency in Bash or Python for setup automation and diagnostics is a plus!

 - Comfortable working independently on customer sites (domestic & international travel)

Must have a valid driver's license to be able to transport equipment to client site and conduct installations

Expected to occasionally lift 30kg+

source_jobs.title: Continuous Improvement Manager (AI Startup)
source_jobs.description:
At Almetra, we’re on a mission to reinvent how factories work.

We help manufacturers, one of the most essential and overlooked sectors of our economy, run better. Every car, wind turbine, medical device, and washing machine comes from a factory. But factories today are running out of people. They struggle with efficiency, safety, and quality, and they desperately need better tools to understand what’s really happening on the shop floor.

That’s where we come in. Almetra builds AI-powered computer vision systems that give manufacturers deep visibility into how their production lines actually run, and the insights to improve them. We don’t sell ads or marketing software. We help real factories solve real problems.

As a Continuous Improvement Engineer, you’ll bridge two worlds: deep operational excellence and cutting-edge AI. You’ll work directly with our customers and internal teams to identify inefficiencies, drive measurable improvement initiatives, and teach leaders and operators how to use AI insights to transform their assembly lines.

If you’ve worked in Lean, CI, or process improvement, and you want to apply that expertise in a fast-moving tech company, this role is for you.




WHY JOIN US

 - Impact that matters. You’ll help manufacturers solve real operational problems, not just implement software.

 - Shape the future of industrial AI. Your domain knowledge will directly influence product evolution and customer outcomes.

 - Trusted advisor status. You’ll work hand-in-hand with customer teams, from operators to plant managers.

 - Career acceleration. You’ll become an expert at translating factory processes into AI-powered improvements across global customers.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Train customers on AI-powered improvement. Create training materials and run workshops that teach customers how to use our AI system to optimize their shop floor.

 - Analyze operations. Review production data and video insights to spot inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and deviations in assembly processes.

 - Generate clear improvement actions. Turn your findings into practical, measurable recommendations for improving customer workflows.

 - Drive implementation. Present improvement measures to customers and guide them on how to apply and scale the changes.

 - Track results. Monitor the impact of implemented actions and adjust recommendations as needed.

 - Be a trusted partner. Build strong relationships with customer teams and support them with Lean and CI best practices.

 - Work cross-functionally. Collaborate with engineering, data science, product, and sales to ensure smooth deployments and strong customer outcomes.

 - Stay current. Keep up with the latest in Lean, CI, manufacturing, and emerging technologies.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Undergraduate or Master’s degree in Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or a related field.

 - 3+ years of experience in Lean manufacturing, CI, or process improvement roles.

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; able to spot trends in complex data sets.

 - A proven track record of designing and implementing successful Lean or CI initiatives.

 - Excellent communication and presentation abilities, capable of explaining complex concepts to non-technical audiences.

 - Fluent verbal and written communication in German and English.

 - Ability to work with operators, engineers, and leadership teams with credibility and empathy.

 - A collaborative mindset and comfort working across Sales, Product, and Engineering.

 - Curiosity about AI, automation, data-driven improvement, and the future of manufacturing.




BONUS POINTS

 - Experience with time studies, MTM, OEE, value stream mapping, or ergonomic assessments.

 - Background in Kaizen facilitation or supporting large-scale CI programs.

 - Familiarity with enterprise software or digital transformation in an industrial environment.

 - Interest in turning CI expertise into scalable playbooks and repeatable AI-powered improvement me

source_jobs.title: Finance Manager
source_jobs.description:
Almetra is an AI company building for manufacturing and industrial automation. We're at Series A and moving fast. This role owns the finance function day-to-day and is built to grow into Head of Finance as we scale. You'll work directly with the founders and have real influence over how we build our financial infrastructure.

What You'll Do

Investor Reporting

 - Own LP/investor reporting cycles: prepare monthly and quarterly packages, manage data rooms, and ensure reporting is accurate and on time

 - Be the internal counterpart for our investors' finance and portfolio teams

Controlling

 - Maintain and improve our controlling setup: P&L, cost center tracking, variance analysis

 - Own the month-end close process and coordinate with our external accountants on VAT filings and year-end

 - Build and maintain internal dashboards that give founders real-time visibility

Forecasting and Planning

 - Own the financial model: rolling forecasts, scenario planning, cash runway visibility

 - Drive the annual budgeting process and keep headcount and OpEx plans current

Finance Operations

 - Work with Juniors / interns and direct their efforts

 - Oversee AP/AR and vendor payment runs

 - Identify process gaps and fix them before they become problems

What You Bring

 - 4+ years in finance, with at least some startup or high-growth company exposure

 - Solid command of German GAAP (HGB); IFRS familiarity is a plus

 - Fluency with financial modeling (Excel/Google Sheets at minimum; planning tools like Mosaic, Runway, or Pigment a plus)

 - Experience owning investor reporting in a VC-backed environment

 - German and English, both working-level or better

 - You operate independently, communicate clearly, and don't need hand-holding

What we offer

Employee Share Options Program for all permanent employees*

An increasing benefits list: currently includes Urban Sports club and quarterly team retreats.

We have a very flat hierarchy, open 360° feedback, and flexible working hours

source_jobs.title: VLM Research Engineer (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a Research Engineer to push the limits of vision-language models for real-world video understanding. You’ll work on applied, state-of-the-art multimodal models and turn them into production pipelines used by customers.


YOUR ROLE

 - Design and adapt vision-language and video models for scene understanding, temporal reasoning and activity / action recognition

 - Build and maintain large-scale training and evaluation pipelines on GPU clusters

 - Curate and augment video-text and action datasets, including synthetic labels and retrieval-based augmentation

 - Develop robust benchmarks for video QA, instruction following and temporal understanding, and use them to drive iterative model improvements

 - Cut and refactor model architectures for efficiency and deployability (compression, pruning, distillation)

 - Deliver production-ready inference pipelines to product and customer teams, working closely with CV, platform and robotics engineers


YOU BRING

 - Completed PhD (or equivalent research track record) in computer vision, machine learning, robotics or a related field

 - Strong background in video-centric deep learning: scene understanding, temporal / activity / action recognition, or video generation

 - Experience training and adapting large vision or VLM models (e.g. InternVL, Qwen-VL, DeepSeek-VL, similar stacks)

 - Proven work with multi-GPU training (PyTorch, distributed, mixed precision) and large-scale datasets

 - Solid engineering habits: clean Python, reproducible experiments, reliable data and training pipelines

 - Track record of moving research into usable systems (demos, internal tools, or productised features) in fast-moving teams


NICE TO HAVE

 - Publications at top-tier venues (CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, NeurIPS, ICLR, etc.) on video, multimodal learning or scene understanding

 - Experience with 3D/4D scene representations, action generation or embodied / sense-plan-act style projects

 - Inference optimisation: quantisation, TensorRT, model distillation, or deployment on constrained hardware

 - Prior experience in a startup or applied research lab environment

What we offer

source_jobs.title: Senior Talent Partner
source_jobs.description:
Almetra is scaling across Commercial and Engineering verticals. We're looking for a Senior Talent Partner to own end-to-end hiring across a vertical while also acting as a strategic Business Partner to the business leaders you support.

This is a high-ownership role. You won't just fill roles; you'll shape how we hire, coach the managers doing the hiring, and build the infrastructure that lets us scale without breaking.



What you'll own

 - Full-cycle recruiting across your verticals, from intake to offer negotiation

 - Sourcing strategy and channel performance across job boards, agencies, and direct pipelines

 - Hiring manager coaching: intake quality, structured interviewing, decision-making calibration

 - Process health: identify bottlenecks, flag risks early, and drive continuous improvement

 - Market intelligence: benchmark compensation, map talent pools, and advise on hiring strategy

 - HRBP partnership with your vertical leads on org design, headcount planning, and people topics as we grow

 - Self-managed prioritisation across competing roles and deadlines, with clear escalation when needed
   
   

What we're looking for

 - 5+ years in in-house talent roles, ideally at a scaling startup or deep-tech company

 - Track record closing senior and technical hires (engineering, ML, commercial, or operations)

 - Comfortable running difficult conversations: pushback on unrealistic briefs, candidate counteroffers, or process breakdowns

 - Sharp process thinking, you notice what's broken before it becomes a problem

 - HRBP or people partner experience, or a strong appetite to grow into it

 - Fluent in English; German is a plus
   
   

#LI-Hybrid

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer (m/f/d) (AI Startup)
source_jobs.description:
Platform Engineer

We're looking for a (Senior) Platform Engineer to own and evolve our infrastructure — with a focus on developer experience and the chance to get hands-on with AI systems.



What you'll do

 - Design and maintain cloud infrastructure on AWS using Terraform

 - Own and improve CI/CD pipelines for fast, safe deployments

 - Manage and scale Kubernetes clusters

 - Improve developer experience — making it easy for teams to ship and debug

 - Implement monitoring, logging and alerting for full observability

 - Configure and optimise CDN infrastructure
   
   

You are

 - 8-10 years of experience in DevOps, SRE or platform engineering roles

 - Expertise in AWS

 - Proficient with Terraform and infrastructure-as-code workflows

 - Experienced in building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins or similar)

 - Solid understanding of Kubernetes — deployment, networking, scaling and troubleshooting

 - Strong sysadmin fundamentals — Linux, networking, storage and configuration management

 - Comfortable working with relational databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL) and time series databases (TimescaleDB, Prometheus)

 - A clear communicator who works well across engineering and data science teams

 - Pragmatic and reliable — you know when to move fast and when to be careful



Nice to have

 - Experience supporting ML infrastructure — model deployment, GPU workloads or training pipelines

 - Background in CDN configuration (CloudFront, Fastly, Cloudflare)

 - Familiarity with streaming data or video processing systems

 - Interest in MLOps and bridging the gap between ML research and production



Why this role matters

You'll keep our systems running smoothly and help the team ship with confidence. And if you're curious about ML, you'll get real exposure to production AI systems.

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a backend engineer who thrives in fast-moving environments and enjoys building robust systems end-to-end. You’ll work across data pipelines, APIs and infrastructure, ensuring our platform runs efficiently and scales cleanly as we grow.

Your role

 - Develop and maintain backend features and APIs for our core platform

 - Improve database performance, reliability and real-time data processing

 - Debug, profile and optimise code to keep latency low and systems stable

 - Collaborate with AI, frontend and product teams to integrate new capabilities

 - Contribute to architectural decisions and shape the evolution of our backend stack

You are

 - 5-7 years’ experience building production-grade backends (Python is a must; additional experience in Go, C++, or Java is valuable)

 - Solid in SQL and data modelling (PostgreSQL or similar)

 - Experienced with REST APIs and cloud environments (AWS preferred)

 - Comfortable working independently and taking ownership of a problem

 - Startup mindset — quick to ship, focused on quality, allergic to bureaucracy

Nice to have

 - Linux debugging and performance tuning

 - Familiarity with orchestration (Kubernetes, Terraform, Ansible)

 - Experience with data pipelines or analytical databases

 - Interest in edge/cloud hybrid architectures

Why this role matters
You’ll help shape how data moves through our system — building fast, reliable infrastructure that powers every customer interaction.

source_jobs.title: Manufacturing Solution Engineer (AI Startup)
source_jobs.description:
At Almetra, we’re on a mission to reinvent how factories work.




We help manufacturers - one of the most essential and overlooked sectors of our economy - run better. Every car, wind turbine, medical device, and washing machine comes from a factory. But factories today are running out of people. They struggle with efficiency, safety, and quality - and they desperately need better tools to understand what’s happening on the shop floor.




That’s where we come in. Almetra builds AI-powered computer vision systems that give manufacturers deep visibility into how their production lines actually run - and the insights to improve them. We don’t sell ads or marketing software. We help real factories solve real problems.




As a Solution Engineer, you’ll bridge two worlds: deep operational expertise and cutting-edge AI. You’ll work directly with our SDRs, AEs, and our Improvement team to land new customers, expand existing ones, and ensure every deployment delivers measurable impact.




If you’ve worked in Continuous Improvement, Lean, or operational excellence, and you want to apply that expertise in a fast-moving tech company then this role is for you.



Why join us

 - Impact that matters. You’ll help manufacturers solve real operational problems, not just implement software.

 - Shape the future of industrial AI. Your domain knowledge will directly influence product evolution and customer outcomes.

 - Cross-functional seat at the table. You’ll partner with Sales, Product, and Engineering to make deals successful and scalable.

 - Career acceleration. You’ll become an expert at translating factory processes into AI-powered improvements across global customers.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Partner with Sales to win deals by bringing credibility in operations, CI, and Lean.

 - Lead discovery sessions to understand current processes, bottlenecks, and improvement opportunities.

 - Translate real-world factory workflows into clear use cases and value stories for prospects.

 - Support pilots and deployments with the Improvement team to demonstrate measurable ROI.

 - Build playbooks for value quantification, before-after analysis, and operational storytelling.

 - Work with multiple stakeholders (ops, quality, engineering, production, CI, plant managers).

 - Help expand existing customers by identifying new use cases and improvement pathways.

 - Feed insights from the field back into Product to shape our roadmap.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years in Continuous Improvement, Lean, Operational Excellence, digital transformation, or industrial engineering.

 - Experience in manufacturing operations, either at a plant or as a consultant.

 - Strong ability to analyze processes, identify bottlenecks, and quantify value.

 - Comfortable with shop-floor environments and talking to operators, engineers, and plant leaders.

 - Clear communicator who can explain complex processes simply and confidently.

 - Ability to run discovery, map workflows, and build compelling business cases.

 - Collaborative mindset and willingness to work closely with Sales, Product, and Engineering.

 - Curious about AI, automation, and the future of manufacturing.


BONUS POINTS

 - Experience with time studies, MTM, value stream mapping, or OEE programs.

 - Background supporting large-scale CI programs or Kaizen events.

 - Familiarity with enterprise software deployments in industrial environments.

 - Interest in turning CI knowledge into scalable, repeatable playbooks.

 - Fluent / Business German speaker

What we offer

Employee Share Options Program for all permanent employees*

An increasing benefits list: currently includes Urban Sports club and quarterly team retreats.

Be on the forefront in defining what artificial intelligence means in manufacturing

Gain hands-on experience in working in an AI-first software company

Supportive and inclusive culture that values diversity and promotes the advancement of underrepresented groups within the company

C

source_jobs.title: Product Owner
source_jobs.description:
We’re hiring a Product Owner to drive delivery and measurable customer outcomes in an industrial software environment. You’ll work closely with customers and internal teams to turn operational needs into clear requirements, shipped features, and improvements that move retention and value.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the backlog and day-to-day execution: write clear tickets, define acceptance criteria, unblock delivery

 - Turn customer problems into actionable requirements and prioritized work packages

 - Run discovery in a pragmatic way: clarify workflows, constraints, data realities, edge cases

 - Coordinate across engineering, customer-facing teams and stakeholders to ship reliably

 - Validate outcomes: ensure what ships works in real customer environments and drives measurable impact

 - Maintain documentation/playbooks so knowledge doesn’t live in people’s heads


YOU ARE

 - 4–7 years experience as Product Owner / Implementation lead in industrial or operational software

 - Strong domain knowledge: factories, production processes, quality, maintenance, throughput, downtime, etc.

 - Comfortable with messy enterprise reality: multiple stakeholders, partial data, changing constraints

 - Able to write high-quality requirements that engineers can implement without guesswork

 - Outcome-oriented: you think in impact metrics, adoption and retention, not feature lists

 - Confident communicator with both operators and engineers


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with MES / ERP / SCADA / industrial analytics tools

 - Basic technical literacy: SQL, APIs, data pipelines (enough to ask good questions)

 - Experience working with AI/analytics products in industrial settings

What we offer

Employee Share Options Program for all permanent employees*
